description:
Welcome to the PINCH Pro Training Program!

This comprehensive training module is designed to empower you with the knowledge and skills to maximize your success on the PINCH platform. Whether you're just getting started or looking to scale your cleaning business, this training will guide you through all the essential features and tools available to you.

You'll learn how to navigate the Opportunity Board to find high-value contracts, manage your teams efficiently, track your jobs in real-time, and monitor your earnings. By mastering these tools, you'll be able to grow your business, increase your revenue, and build stronger relationships with property managers.

This training is your gateway to transforming your cleaning business and achieving unprecedented success with PINCH. Let's get started!

video.subtitle: How to use Job Tracker
video.description:
Welcome to the Job Tracker training. In this video, you will learn how to use the Job Tracker step by step.

Before starting, enter your mobile phone number and tap Location Shared. Then, click "Start Job" to begin.

Start by taking the before photos. The first required area is the front door, with the property number clearly visible. Take photos of the space as it is before starting the service. Make sure to capture multiple angles and close-up detail to fully document the condition.

Complete the checklist and submit your photos to close the job. The checklist defines areas, photos, and completion standards.

Once cleaning is complete, take clear after photos of each required area to document the finished work. These photos are required for job validation and payment approval, so ensure they are complete, clear, and accurate.

Make sure all areas of the property show the green icon in each section. When all photos are complete, click Finish Job. If everything looks good, select 'Finish Job,' and your job is complete.

video.subtitle: Money Section
video.description:
In this video, you'll learn how to use the Money section in PINCH, a key tool to manage your earnings and stay in control of your payments.

Monthly routine services are typically billed by the PINCH finance team at the beginning of each month. Tag work or A La Carte work is invoiced the day after the work is completed.

The Money section is important because it gives you clear visibility into how much you're earning, which payments are in progress, and when you'll receive your money. This visibility helps you better manage your cash flow and make smarter decisions for your business.

Here, you can find all your payment-related information in one place.

Inside the Money section, you'll be able to see: Your earnings from each completed job, The status of your payments (pending, in progress, or paid), Estimated payment dates and Your full transaction history.

Checking this section regularly will help you track your income and make sure everything is on track. You do not need to provide an invoice to PINCH for work you complete.

Remember, having full control over your income is key to growing your cleaning business in a sustainable way.

video.subtitle: Teams Section
video.description:
In this video, you'll learn how to create and manage your teams in PINCH, and why this section is key to running your operations efficiently.

The Teams section is important because it helps you organize your workforce, assign jobs more effectively, give your team members access to the correct job tracker links, and scale your business without losing control.

Inside the Teams section, you'll be able to: Create teams and organize your cleaners into groups. Start by adding the name and the phone number of your team member. Make sure that phone number actually belongs to your cleaner, because they will always receive notifications there and will be able to review all the jobs assigned to them. Click the save button to save the individual.

You can group your cleaners by crew, location, availability, or type of service. Only groups can be assigned to jobs that require a larger number of team members. Go to Groups and create one, then give it a name so you can identify it, and assign the number of members you need for that group. This way, notifications will be sent to them as a group.

This allows you to manage your team in a structured way instead of assigning jobs one by one or having to send out a high volume of text or Whatsapp messages.

Creating teams helps you clearly see how many people you have available for each project and avoid overbooking or under-assigning jobs.

When assigning jobs, instead of selecting individual cleaners every time, you can assign an entire team, making the process faster and more efficient. This also helps ensure consistency in your service, since the same team can handle similar types of jobs or properties.

Make sure to keep your teams updated regularly so your assignments reflect your real availability.

moduleHeader.subtitle: Unlock Bigger Cleaning Contracts with Partner Membership

video.subtitle: Unlock Bigger Cleaning Contracts with Partner Membership
video.description:
Congratulations! You're now positioned to access bigger contracts with your Partner Membership status. This advanced membership tier provides you with dedicated support and resources designed to help you win larger, more valuable contracts.

As a Partner, you gain access to:

1. Dedicated Sales Representative: You'll be assigned a dedicated Sales Rep who understands your business, your capacity, and your growth goals. They'll provide personalized guidance on which opportunities align with your strengths.

2. Strategic Planning: Work with your Sales Agent in regular weekly meetings to develop a strategy for targeting higher-value contracts. You'll discuss pricing, positioning, and overall market approach.

3. Priority Opportunities: As a Partner, you'll receive early access to premium contracts and opportunities that match your growth trajectory.

4. Enhanced Support: Access to advanced training, resources, and business development tools that help you scale effectively.

5. Growth Acceleration: Partner membership is designed to help you grow faster by focusing on bigger contracts and strategic opportunities.

To maximize your Partner membership benefits, stay organized, responsive, and proactive. Keep your team availability clear, update your profile regularly, and engage actively with your Sales Rep. The stronger your partnership with PINCH, the bigger the contracts you'll unlock.

video.subtitle: Opportunity Board
video.description:
In this video, you'll learn how to use the PINCH Opportunity Board, a key tool to find new cleaning contracts and grow your business.

The Opportunity Board is important because it gives you visibility into real opportunities posted by PINCH in different cities, helping you identify projects that match your location, team size, and the services you offer.

This is where PINCH publishes cleaning projects from property managers and commercial clients.

Opportunities vary in size and scope. Some are small, one-time jobs, while others are long-term, recurring contracts.

To access the Opportunity Board, log in to the portal using your credentials. Go to the opportunities link at the top. Here, you'll see a list of available projects.

Each opportunity includes important information about the service type. These include Turnover, One time services, Multifamily, Common Area, Routine Services, Student Housing...and many more.

While reviewing an opportunity, you will find the type of property, location, start date, estimated project duration and estimated annual contract value.

Review each opportunity carefully and focus on projects that match your city, the size of your team, and the services you already provide.

The Opportunity Board is updated constantly as our sales team closes new deals. New projects are added as PINCH receives client requests.

Checking it frequently will help you spot new opportunities early and increase your chances of applying to contracts that fit your business.

video.subtitle: What Now - Member / Elite
video.description:
Congratulations! Your account is now ready. Now let's walk through what you should do next to start getting your first jobs on PINCH.

Before anything else, make sure you complete your background check. This is a required step to access job opportunities and start working with PINCH. Without it, you won't be eligible for contracts.

Once your profile is ready, go to the Opportunity Board. This is where PINCH publishes real cleaning contracts from property managers and commercial clients.

Here you'll find the location, type of service, frequency, and estimated value of each opportunity. Focus on the ones that match your area and your team capacity.

The faster you act, the higher your chances of winning contracts. Top providers check the board daily, evaluate opportunities quickly, and send proposals consistently.

Make sure your profile is always updated. This helps you get selected faster, build trust with clients, and access better opportunities.

Use the tools available in your account to create proposals, organize your team, and manage your jobs. Everything is designed to help you grow and win larger contracts.

Also, make sure to stay informed through our newsletter, The Cleaners Edge, and keep an eye on all PINCH communications. Don't forget to check your spam folder and make sure our emails are marked as safe so you never miss new opportunities or communications.

You're now ready to get your first contract. Go to your Opportunity Board, take action, and start growing your business with PINCH.

video.subtitle: What Now - Partner Membership
video.description:
Congratulations! You're now a Partner on PINCH. This means you now have access to a more advanced level of support designed to help you win larger contracts.

Your first step is to schedule your Partner onboarding session. You'll receive an email with a calendar link to book it. In this session, you'll learn how we'll work together and get connected with your dedicated Sales Rep to start closing bigger contracts. 

Once your Sales Agent is assigned, you'll work together on opportunities. Following a structured approach is key to staying organized and successfully closing higher-value contracts.

Once you complete your onboarding session, you'll start having meetings once a week to guidance on which opportunities to prioritize based on your capacity, location, and growth goals.

As a Partner, your focus should be on being ready to take on larger jobs. Make sure your team is organized, your availability is clear, and you are responsive when new opportunities are shared with you.

In each session, you'll work with your sales agent to improve your pricing, positioning, and overall strategy when going after contracts. This is a key advantage of being a Partner.

The Opportunity Board is your second channel to win contracts. Focusing on higher-value contracts and opportunities aligned with your growth.

Also, make sure to stay informed through our newsletter, The Cleaners Edge, and keep an eye on all PINCH communications. Don't forget to check your spam or junk folder and make sure our emails are marked as safe so you never miss important updates or opportunities.

You're now positioned to grow faster and close bigger contracts with PINCH. Stay proactive, stay responsive, and take full advantage of the support available to you.

moduleHeader.subtitle: Job Tracker Protects Your Work and Your Payment

video.subtitle: Job Tracker Protects Your Work and Your Payment
video.description:
At PINCH, every job must be recorded in the Job Tracker. This applies to all types of cleaning services and jobs that are completed, including nightly routine services.

Job Tracker is the system we use to document each cleaning and confirms that the work was completed at the property based on the scope of work.

Job Tracker is not just a tool. It protects your work and validates the service you provide.

The assigned cleaner must complete all items included in the job scope for the job to be considered completed.

When Job Tracker is used correctly, it helps you strengthen your professional reputation, demonstrates the quality of your service, and protects you from being accused of not completing areas that may have been dirtied by another vendor after leaving a property or job site.

It also keeps the payment process moving smoothly and reduces potential claims after the service.

Every job is reviewed and approved by the PINCH Operations Team. If the job is well documented, the payment process moves forward quickly. If there are no photos or records, the process pauses until the job is validated by on-site staff. 

Using Job Tracker correctly is a fundamental part of delivering great customer service. It protects your work, your reputation, and your payment.

video.subtitle: Job Section
video.description:
In this video, you'll learn how to use the Jobs section in your Pro PINCH account.

The Jobs section is where you assign work to your team. It's important to understand that jobs can only be assigned to groups of cleaners, not individual team members.

Before assigning a job, make sure you have your teams properly created in the Teams section. This will allow you to organize your operations more efficiently.

When you enter the Jobs section, you will see the jobs in the Appointments section, which are upcoming jobs to be completed. Here, you can assign the group you decide will work on that job.

This way, the job will be ready to be completed by the assigned group.

And how can you review the work they've done? You can click on "Go to Tracker Link" to see live before-and-after photos of their work and validate that the service was completed correctly.

Remember that all cleaners must use their Job Tracker to validate that the work was completed, ensuring the payment is processed smoothly and faster without any issues.

To summarize, always assign your jobs through the platform, make sure your teams are well organized, and ensure your cleaners are using the Job Tracker for every job. This will help you run a more efficient operation and grow your business with PINCH.

message: You’ve just taken a big step forward. You’re no longer just doing jobs, you’re building a business ready to scale and win bigger contracts. Complete your background check to unlock real opportunities. This is where growth begins.
